recapPrompt.prompt: Why do base cases matter in dynamic programming?

choices.label: They give the first known answers to build from
choices.feedback: Correct. Base cases are the little starting answers the rest of the table depends on.

choices.label: They sort the table from smallest to largest
choices.feedback: Not this one. Base cases are about starting knowledge, not sorting.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Explain in your own words why saving smaller answers can make a hard problem faster.

practiceChallenge.title: Mastery check: rebuild the DP clues
practiceChallenge.description: Complete the helper so it returns the main clue for counting DP, minimum DP, and top-plus-left grid DP.
hints: Minimum DP chooses the cheaper earlier path.
Grid DP can use top plus left.
practiceChallenge.successMessage: Pass. You rebuilt the main dynamic-programming clues from memory.
practiceChallenge.retryMessage: Needs practice. Recheck counting, minimum, and grid DP clues.
